articles.title: Switzerland Q4 GDP +0.1% vs +0.2% q/q expected
articles.snippet: Prior -0.5%; revised to -0.4%Swiss economic output bounces back in the final quarter of last year, after a drag in the third quarter. The decline was largely due to a big fall in the chemical and pharmaceutical industry, but that has now rebounded (+1.9%) in Q4 2025. Domestic demand conditions (+0.5%) held up and was supportive in the final quarter, helping to push up growth.Looking to the overall 2025 picture, Swiss GDP grew by 1.4% on the year. And that marks an improvement from the 1.2% readi

articles.title: France February preliminary CPI +1.0% vs +0.8% y/y expected
articles.snippet: Prior +0.3%HICP +1.1% vs +0.7% y/y expectedPrior +0.4%The bigger jump in the annual estimates is largely tied to base effects, as electricity prices had fallen more sharply in February 2025. That being said, the monthly estimates also show a rebound in prices with headline inflation up 0.8% in February. That follows the 0.3% decline in January. The bump this month is the biggest monthly gain in prices since February 2024.Looking at the bigger picture, food price inflation picked up a little and

articles.title: France Q4 final GDP +0.2% vs +0.2% q/q prelim
articles.snippet: Prior +0.5%French Q4 GDP is confirmed at +0.2% q/q, reflecting slight growth in the final quarter of last year. This leads to overall GDP being 0.9% for the year 2025, which is slightly softer than the 1.1% growth in 2024.Looking at the overall breakdown for the year, domestic demand conditions held up well and contributed 0.8% to GDP. Meanwhile, inventory changes accounted for 0.7% growth. And all of that is offset slightly by net foreign trade, which saw a drag of 0.6% to growth in 2025. This

articles.title: Expensify Announces Q4 and Full Year Fiscal 2025 Results
articles.snippet: The company generated $20.1 million in operating cash flow and $19.9 million in free cash flow in fiscal year 2025 SAN FRANCISCO — Expensify, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXFY), a payments superapp that helps individuals and businesses around the world simplify the way they…

articles.title: SMCP Sales Slip in Q4 as Exit From BHV Weighs on France
articles.snippet: SMCP’s French operations were hit by exiting all SGM properties, while international markets and a full-price sales strategy boosted full-year profitability.

SMCP’s French operations were hit by exiting all SGM properties, while international markets and a full-price sales strategy boosted full-year profitability.
